sections.title: Let's get started
sections.subtitle: A couple of quick questions before we dive in.

questions.text: Is this a gift for someone, or is it for yourself?

questions.placeholder: e.g. Emma, The Johnsons, Mum & Dad

questions.text: Tell us about them — who are they? What do they love? Any memories, places, or in-jokes we should know about?
questions.placeholder: e.g. Emma and Jack have been together 8 years. They met at a pub quiz in Brighton. They're obsessed with road trips and always argue about who picks the music...
questions.hint: The more you share, the more personal the game will feel. Nothing is too random.

questions.text: Tell us about your group — who's playing? What's your thing? Any shared memories, places, or in-jokes?
questions.placeholder: e.g. Four friends from uni, obsessed with football and terrible at cooking. We've been doing a pub quiz every Thursday for five years...
questions.hint: The more detail you give us, the more the game will feel like it was made just for you.

sections.title: How hands-on do you want to be?
sections.subtitle: You can always change your mind as you go.

questions.text: How would you like to build your game?

options.label: Keep it simple
options.sublabel: Give us the basics and our AI handles the rest. Done in under 5 minutes.

options.label: Start me off, I'll adjust
options.sublabel: We'll pre-fill suggestions — you tweak what matters to you. Recommended.

options.label: I want full control
options.sublabel: You fill in every property name, card, and detail yourself.

sections.title: The world of your game
sections.subtitle: This is where your game gets its personality.

questions.text: What is your game's world or theme?
questions.placeholder: e.g. Taylor Swift eras, Bristol in the 90s, our relationship, Game of Thrones
questions.hint: Think of this as the universe your game lives in. It shapes everything — the names, the cards, the mood.

questions.text: Any places that should be on the board?
questions.sublabel: Streets, pubs, cities, venues, landmarks — places that mean something to you or the recipient. These become your properties.
questions.hint: Add as many or as few as you like. We'll fill the rest with places that match your theme.
questions.placeholder_name: e.g. The Anchor Pub, Brighton seafront

questions.text: Anything else that should inspire the game?
questions.sublabel: Inside jokes, favourite things, shared obsessions, specific people — anything goes.
questions.placeholder: e.g. Dave always loses at Monopoly and we never let him forget it. Our dog is called Biscuit.

sections.title: How should it look?
sections.subtitle: Set the visual direction for your board.

questions.text: Pick a visual style for your game

options.label: Retro & Vintage
options.description: Warm tones, serif type, old-school charm

questions.freeform_label: Or describe it in your own words
questions.freeform_placeholder: e.g. dark academia, cottagecore, 80s neon, something that feels like a Wes Anderson film

questions.sublabel: Pick up to 3 colours. We'll build the board's palette around these.
questions.generate_prompt: Suggest a 3-colour palette that fits the theme and style. Return as hex codes with brief labels.

sections.title: Name your game
sections.subtitle: The details that go on the board itself.

questions.text: What would you like to call the game?
questions.placeholder: e.g. Swiftopolis, Bristololy, The Love Game
questions.generate_prompt: Suggest a creative, punchy game name based on the theme. Offer 3 options.

questions.placeholder: e.g. Swifties, Bristols, Love Bucks, Dragons
questions.hint: This replaces 'pounds' or 'dollars' on the board and money cards.
questions.generate_prompt: Suggest a fun, thematic currency name for the game. Offer 3 options.

sections.title: Who's playing?
sections.subtitle: Characters you add here will appear on the board and in the game.

questions.text: Who should feature in this game?
questions.sublabel: Think people, pets, or fictional characters that matter to the recipient.
questions.placeholder: Type a name and press Enter

sections.title: Configure your board
sections.subtitle: Fill in what matters to you. Leave anything blank and we'll generate it.

questions.sublabel: These are the 22 properties on your board, grouped by colour. Edit any, leave blanks for AI to fill.
questions.generate_prompt: Generate names for all blank properties in the grid using the theme and sentimental places.

questions.sublabel: Stations appear at the 4 midpoints of the board.
questions.generate_prompt: Generate 4 station names fitting the theme.

questions.sublabel: Utilities are the two 'infrastructure' squares on the board.
questions.generate_prompt: Generate 2 utility names fitting the theme.

questions.sublabel: There are two tax squares on the board. Name them to fit your theme.
questions.placeholder: e.g. Spotify Royalties, The Hangover Tax
questions.generate_prompt: Generate 2 tax square names fitting the theme.

questions.sublabel: The four corners: Go, Jail, Free Parking, and Go to Jail.
hints: GO — where everyone starts and collects on each pass
Jail / Just Visiting — the holding square
Free Parking — the middle rest stop
Go to Jail — the trouble square
questions.generate_prompt: Generate themed names for the 4 corner squares.

questions.sublabel: We've generated 16 Chance cards for your theme. Edit any you'd like to personalise further.
questions.generate_prompt: Generate 16 Chance cards for the property trading game theme.

questions.sublabel: 16 Treasury cards, pre-generated for your theme. Edit freely.
questions.generate_prompt: Generate 16 Treasury cards for the property trading game theme.

sections.title: Almost there
sections.subtitle: Last chance to add anything before we show you your game.

questions.text: Anything else you'd like us to know?
questions.placeholder: e.g. Please avoid any references to X. The recipient hates the colour orange. Make it funny.
questions.hint: This is your chance to give us any final instructions, restrictions, or requests.

questions.sublabel_digital: You'll receive a companion page URL and downloadable print files.
questions.sublabel_physical: Your fully printed game will be manufactured and shipped to you.

preview.title: Here's a taste of your game
preview.description: We've generated one quadrant of your board based on your answers. The full game will be built after you complete your order.
